_doc: Machine-readable release baselines for v0.2-rc (M2.8 / M2.8.1). Contains current locked / snapshot values; `retired` section captures M2.x ARAM SHA evolutions only. Not a complete historical baseline registry — pre-M2 retired baselines live in docs/history/M0-M2-passes.md. Three current classifications: identity_gated (any drift = regression), behavior_gated (numeric thresholds documenting policy), documentary_snapshot (informational; expected to shift on declared milestones). M3.1 migration: M2_ATOM_128_SINE_PCM_SHA256 and M2_ATOM_64_SINE_PCM_SHA256 moved from this file's documentary_snapshot to baselines/m3.json's identity_gated section per the SPEC §16.9 atom PCM stability amendment (M3.0); see _migrated_to_m3 field.

identity_gated.name: M1_DRIVER_CODE_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Originally locked at M2.0 with value 671ee21eb...4b2bcfe but no test asserted the literal value — the M2.0 bootstrap-token fix (`fix(driver): seed dp_last_token from $F4 at init`, consultant M2.0 #1) added bytes to the M1 driver and the SHA shifted unobserved. M2.8.1 added `m1_driver_code_sha_matches_locked_baseline` to pin the current value via this entry; further drift now fails the test.

identity_gated.name: M2_CANONICAL_SEQUENCE_TOTAL_TICKS
identity_gated._note: sum-of-WAIT-operands; semantic locked at M2.4. Wall-elapsed tick count is `M2_CANONICAL_SEQUENCE_ELAPSED_TICKS` = 254.

identity_gated.name: M2_CANONICAL_SEQUENCE_ELAPSED_TICKS
identity_gated.rule: wall-elapsed tick count under SPEC §14.3 wait-decrement-before-opcode-read; END opcode reads on tick 254 for canonical fixture (= 249 + 5 WAITs).

behavior_gated.name: M2_AUDIBILITY_FLOORS
behavior_gated.rule: per-channel audible floor for at-least-one-channel-audible per SPEC §21

behavior_gated.name: M2_SOURCE_STEP_ZCR_RATIO_FLOOR
behavior_gated.rule: post.right.zcr / pre.right.zcr >= 1.5 in canonical source-step combined fixture

behavior_gated.name: M2_MODULE_CAP_BYTES
behavior_gated.rule: SPEC §15.6: module.bin > 32 KiB triggers ModuleTooLarge; <= 32 KiB ok

documentary_snapshot.name: M2_DRIVER_CODE_OBSERVED_BYTES
documentary_snapshot.snapshot_at: M2.5 (canonical M2 fixture, current asar version)
documentary_snapshot._note: informational; not gated. Expected to shift at M3 when driver gains features.

documentary_snapshot.name: M2_ATOM_128_SINE_BRR_SHA256
documentary_snapshot._note: M3 BRR-encoder-quality work expected to invalidate; reclassify as snapshot per consultant #26.

documentary_snapshot.name: M2_ATOM_64_SINE_BRR_SHA256
documentary_snapshot._note: M3 BRR encoder work expected to invalidate.

documentary_snapshot.name: M2_ATOM_128_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_SCORE
documentary_snapshot._note: M2.2-era encoder-internal score: |first_loop_decoded - last_decoded| with M2.2 BRR encoder + force_filter_0_first_block, no phase rotation. Same formula as SPEC §10.6 loop_click_abs (confirmed at M3.1 reconciliation). Documentary; the authoritative M3 entries are in baselines/m3.json (`M3_ATOM_128_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_ABS_PRE_M3 = 1197` pre-rotation, `…_PHASE_ROTATION = 0` post-M3.3 rotation). Retired from gating but kept here for historical traceability.

documentary_snapshot.name: M2_ATOM_64_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_SCORE
documentary_snapshot._note: Same envelope as the 128 atom; higher absolute value reflects shorter cycle length. M3.3 phase rotation drops this to 0 (rotation_offset=48); see baselines/m3.json::M3_ATOM_64_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_ABS_PHASE_ROTATION.

retired.name: M2_MULTI_VOICE_ARAM_SHA256_M24
retired.reason: driver bytes shifted (KOFF-clear-before-KON, init_kon_mask, jmp main_loop fix; sequence_addr now points at payload not header)

retired.name: M2_MULTI_VOICE_ARAM_SHA256_M23
retired.reason: sequence_data region added between source directory and sample BRR pool
